sections.title: 🛌  Sleep
items: Non-negotiable: 7.5–8 hrs / night. Sacrifice study, never sleep — your brain consolidates during sleep.
No screen 1 hour before bed. Notes on paper > phone for the last 30 minutes.
Wake the same time every day (yes, including Sundays).

sections.title: 🍳  Food
items: Heavy breakfast (eggs / dal / paratha) → fuels morning practical block.
Light lunch (chapati + sabzi + dahi) → no rice/sugar crash at 2 pm.
Snack between sessions: nuts, fruit. Not biscuits/Maggi.
No caffeine after 4 pm or sleep suffers.

sections.title: 💪  Exercise
items: 20-min walk every evening, non-negotiable. Outdoors, no phone.
2× per week: 30-min strength (push-ups, squats, planks at home).
1× per week: 1-hour activity you enjoy (sport, cycling, anything).

sections.title: 🧠  Study-session structure
items: Pomodoro: 50 min focus + 10 min break. Use your own Tympeak app.
After 3 pomodoros → 30-min break (real food, real walk).
Max 4 hours continuous study. Then a HARD break — eat / nap / walk.
Standing while reading Audit/SM theory = better recall.

sections.title: 🚨  Burn-out warning signs
items: · Can't recall what you read 5 min ago → brain is full, REST.
· Snapping at family → emotional reserves depleted, REST.
· Sleep < 7 hrs for 3 nights → recovery debt, REST.
· Mock score drops sharply → you're tired, not stupid, REST.
Burn-out protocol: 1 full day off + 7+ hrs sleep + light social time. Then back to it.

sections.title: 🎯  Mock-day protocol
items: 1. Night before: light dinner, 8 hrs sleep, lay out everything (pen, watch, water).
2. Morning: normal breakfast, no last-min reading.
3. Sit at desk at 9:55 am sharp. Take the 3-hour mock under exam conditions: timer, no phone, no breaks.
4. After: 30-min break. Then check answers (DO NOT skip this step).
5. Same day: re-do every wrong question. Tomorrow is too late.

sections.title: 💪  Mental model for exam day
items: You are not trying to score 100%. You are trying to score 50%+ to pass.
In each paper: minimum 40%. Aggregate 50%+ across all 3 papers.
Solve the easy 60 marks first. Then the medium 30. Skip the hard 10 unless time permits.
When stuck, write SOMETHING. ICAI gives step-marks for method even if the final answer is wrong.
Presentation matters. Clean working notes, underlined final answers, neat handwriting in Audit/SM.
